subject: Mathematics
title: The Liar Paradox
description: Learn The Liar Paradox in one minute.

hook.text: 'This sentence is false.' If it's true, then it's false. If it's false, then it's true.

buildup.text: The Liar Paradox dates back to the ancient Greek Epimenides, who said all Cretans are liars—while being Cretan.

discovery.text: The paradox shows that self-reference can break logical systems—a sentence about itself creates a loop.

twist.text: Bertrand Russell found a similar paradox in set theory: the set of all sets that don't contain themselves.

climax.text: These paradoxes forced mathematicians to rebuild the foundations of logic in the 20th century.

punchline.text: One sentence broke logic. Rebuilding took decades.

quiz.question: What makes the Liar Paradox a paradox?
